mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5 percent signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of months where UNRATE falls below 6.5, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic momentum that lifts cyclical equity sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC (< 0.04) over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day returns are lower when both INDPRO and HOUST are above trend compared to when only one is above trend.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.25 percent during a tightening cycle increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, or the forward return differential between high and low rate months is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which lowers input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with rising INDPRO are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts exceeding 1050 thousand units signal robust consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window as wealth effects translate into broader market buying.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months where HOUST surpasses 1050, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising nonfarm payrolls and a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting labor market strength, which historically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings growth accelerates without inflationary pressure.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative Rank IC (< 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are lower when PAYEMS rises and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% compared to periods with rising payrolls but higher rates.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized during an early-cycle recovery phase confirms bottoming economic activity and triggers institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, or the forward return differential between high-growth and low-growth quarters is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
